title: Malacochaete tatora
taxonomy: Malacochaete tatora is classified within the family Cladoniaceae, which is a group of lichens commonly known as the "crustose lichens". This classification places Malacochaete tatora among other species of lichens that share similar characteristics. The genus Malacochaete is part of the larger class Lecanoromycetes, which includes a wide variety of lichens. Malacochaete tatora's classification is further defined by its specific order, which is Lecanorales. This classification is based on the lichen's physical characteristics and its association with specific fungi and algae.

intro: Malacochaete tatora is a species of liverwort, a type of non-vascular plant that belongs to the class Marchantiophyta. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. The bioactive constituents of Malacochaete tatora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
